title: Chassis diagnostic
description: Structured, agent-routable diagnostic emitted by Chassis gates, validators, and drift checks. Every finding surfaced via --json conforms to this schema. The ruleId + violated.convention pair binds the diagnostic to an ADR; the fix.applicability enum tells an agent whether to auto-apply.

ruleId.description: Stable identifier for the rule this diagnostic violates. Format: DOMAIN-NNN (e.g. CHASSIS-001, GATE-EXPORT-03). Must resolve to an ADR whose enforces[].rule == ruleId.

severity.description: error = hard-fail in non-advisory mode; warning = visible but non-blocking; info = observation only.

message.description: Human-readable one-line summary. Keep under 160 characters; detail belongs in detail.

violated.description: Link to the convention (ADR) this diagnostic enforces. Required when ruleId is bound to an ADR.

convention.description: ADR id (e.g. ADR-0007). The ADR's enforces[] array must include the ruleId.

docs.description: Stable anchor URL pointing at prose explaining the rule. Typically a fragment-linked docs/chassis/guides/*.md or an ADR file.

fix.description: Optional remediation hint. When applicability='automatic', the patch field should be populated; this is validated separately by the binding-link gate rather than in JSON Schema (Draft-07 if/then/else is forbidden by schemas/portability rules for cross-language compatibility).

applicability.description: automatic = tool can apply without human review; suggested = tool proposes, human confirms; manual-only = requires human judgment.

description.description: Short natural-language description of the fix.

patch.description: Optional unified-diff or script fragment implementing the fix. Should be populated when applicability=automatic.

location.description: Where the violation lives. Path is always required; range is optional.

detail.description: Free-form structured extension. Gate-specific fields live here (e.g. baseline counts, affected item lists).

examples.message: ruleId 'NAMING-RUST-FN-CAMEL' emitted by [naming-rust] does not resolve to any ADR's enforces[]

fix.description: Either add the rule to an ADR's enforces[] array or rename the emitting site to use a registered ruleId.
